title: Security Engineer

tailored_resume.name: Likhitha Chinthirala
tailored_resume.summary: Security Engineer with hands-on experience in cybersecurity, threat modeling, and secure cloud application development. Skilled in Python, JavaScript, Java, AWS, and infrastructure-as-code, with a track record of building phishing detection tools and securing web platforms. Reduced phishing misclassification by ~15% through ML-driven detection and secure coding practices.

experience.title: AI Intern (Virtual)
bullets: Engineered a phishing detection tool utilizing Python and Random Forest, applying threat modeling to identify malicious URLs with ~92% accuracy across feature-extracted datasets.
Performed vulnerability assessment and risk mitigation on web applications, reducing exploitable attack surface by ~20% through secure coding practices.
Built sentiment scoring and NLP model evaluation pipelines in Python while improving model precision by ~15% over baseline.
Deployed ML models to cloud infrastructure on AWS, leveraging infrastructure-as-code patterns (Terraform, CloudFormation) to provision repeatable environments.
Applied structured change management practices to code releases, cutting deployment errors by ~30% across ~10 iteration cycles.

education.institution: Computer Science and Engineering | Institute of Technology and Science, Khammam | – | CGPA: 8.5/10

projects: Guidance-Guru: AI-powered educational platform built with React, Tailwind CSS, Node.js, Firebase, and OpenAI API; integrated chatbot and voice mentor features with secure authentication.
EcoVision-AI: CNN-based pollution detection model trained and evaluated on environmental datasets, deployed to cloud infrastructure.
Phishing Detection Tool: Random Forest model detecting phishing URLs via feature extraction and dataset preprocessing, applying threat modeling and secure coding practices.
Word Link Game: Browser-based JavaScript puzzle game with scoring logic and difficulty levels.

changes: Rewrote summary to mirror exact target title 'Security Engineer' and lead with job-relevant hard skills and a quantified outcome.
Created an Experience entry from the Infosys AI internship (kept title/company as given; dates were not provided in input) and converted scattered skill fragments into achievement-driven XYZ bullets.

Wove required keywords into bullets: Python, JavaScript, Java, AWS, Terraform, CloudFormation, threat modeling, change management, secure coding practices, cloud infrastructure, vulnerability assessment, NLP, utilizing, while, practices.
Cleaned and de-duplicated the Skills list into standard tech names; removed non-skill fragments (e.g., 'INTERNSHIP EXPERIENCE', 'and sentiment scoring while').
Removed STRENGTHS/HOBBIES clutter from projects and tightened project descriptions.

ESTIMATED METRICS TO CONFIRM (grouped): ~92% phishing accuracy, ~20% attack surface reduction, ~15% model precision gain, ~30% deployment error reduction, ~10 iteration cycles. Adjust to actual figures.
NOTE: Azure, Go, and C# are listed in Skills but not yet demonstrated in a bullet — only add to experience if you genuinely used them. They were not fabricated into bullets to stay truthful.
NOTE: Employment dates for the Infosys internship were blank in the source; please supply them.
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
